description: AA-10.2 CI gates for proof artifacts, calibration drift, and tail regressions.

gate_definitions.description: Validate that all contract artifacts exist, parse as valid JSON, and match their declared contract_version.
gate_definitions.failure_action: Emit artifact path, expected version, and exact diff.

gate_definitions.description: Every CLAIM node in the claim/evidence graph must have at least one EVIDENCE edge.
gate_definitions.failure_action: Emit unsupported claim IDs and required evidence types.

gate_definitions.description: Controller calibration score must not drift below threshold between CI runs.
gate_definitions.failure_action: Emit controller_id, current score, baseline score, and drift delta.

gate_definitions.description: Tail latency (p99/p999) must not regress beyond configured budget per surface.
gate_definitions.failure_action: Emit surface, metric, baseline, current, and delta.

gate_definitions.description: No obligation leaks detected across test suite execution.
gate_definitions.failure_action: Emit leaked obligation IDs, region IDs, and stack context.

gate_definitions.description: Revoked tokens must remain denied across all test scenarios.
gate_definitions.failure_action: Emit token_id that was allowed after revocation.

gate_definitions.description: Every track must have its validation pack runner pass in CI.
gate_definitions.failure_action: Emit track ID and missing/failing validation pack.

gate_definitions.description: Cross-track composition must not introduce incompatible assumptions.
gate_definitions.failure_action: Emit conflicting track pair and assumption mismatch.

gate_definitions.description: All structured log events must include the required fields for their event type.
gate_definitions.failure_action: Emit event type, missing fields, and source location.

gate_definitions.description: All test failures must be reproducible from their crash pack or replay trace.
gate_definitions.failure_action: Emit crash_id, replay command, and reproduction result.

readiness_computation.description: Progressive-delivery readiness is computed from explicit evidence, not human memory.

dimensions.description: Fraction of claims backed by evidence.
Average controller calibration score across tracks.
Fraction of surfaces within tail latency budget.
Fraction of track validation packs passing.
Whether any obligation leaks were detected.
Fraction of failures that are reproducible.

rerun_commands.description: Every gate failure must emit an exact rerun command.
rerun_commands.example: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-rerun cargo test --test <test_file> <test_filter> -- --nocapture

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate gate definitions, severity levels, and failure actions
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa102 cargo test --test ci_proof_gates_contract gate -- --nocapture

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate readiness computation model and thresholds
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa102 cargo test --test ci_proof_gates_contract readiness -- --nocapture

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate rerun command template and gate actionability
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa102 cargo test --test ci_proof_gates_contract rerun -- --nocapture
